title: Chapter 2593 - 51: Red Sun_2
content:
Chapter 2593: Chapter 51: Red Sun_2

Mo Hua was bound by the fateful evilness and couldn’t kill anyone.

Even though the Big Tiger was a powerful Monster Beast, it couldn’t possibly kill that many.

Thus, Mo Hua could only guide the Big Tiger to avoid the encirclement and pursuit of the Great Wilderness Sect disciples.

However, the situation was not optimistic.

Prior to this, Mo Hua understood the power of the "Taoist Soldiers" system, which prevailed over weakness.

In fact, in the previous sword discussion, it was the nascent form of the meticulously prepared and disciplined "Heavenly Pride Taoist Soldiers" from the Taixu Gate that secured an initial advantage.

But it was only when being blocked and pursued by the Great Wilderness Sect disciples using the Taoist Soldiers tactics, that Mo Hua truly experienced the strength of the cultivator army system.

The disciples of the Great Wilderness Sect gathered and formed formations, forcing Mo Hua and the Big Tiger to flee in all directions, facing peril at every turn.

Mo Hua could only temporarily hold them in check with spells and the Formation.

Apart from that, they relied on the Big Tiger’s valor, using its fangs and claws to tear through defenses and escape the encirclement.

Fortunately, the Big Tiger was a Second Grade Peak tiger Monster Beast, a mighty one among monsters, and even without using its mysterious abilities, it was incredibly fast when running at full speed.

Thus, the human and the tiger struggled to survive in the gaps between arrows and blades, breaking through heavy encirclements.

Finally, after fighting for an unknown amount of time, Mo Hua temporarily shook off the pursuers and spotted a canyon.

On the other side of the canyon was a cliff.

Beyond the cliff was the camp of the Taoist Soldiers Court.

Crossing the canyon and leaping over the cliff would completely break free from the Great Wilderness Sect’s encirclement.

Mo Hua didn’t hesitate any longer and patted the Big Tiger’s neck.

The Big Tiger understood and immediately transformed into a gale, rushing towards the other side of the canyon.

But as soon as they entered the canyon, a squad of ten cultivators suddenly emerged from one side.

These ten were clad in golden patterned beast armor, excellently crafted and heavy, with a deep and focused aura, and their cultivation was uniformly at the Foundation Establishment Peak.

They were clearly elite disciples, carefully selected from the Great Wilderness Sect.

They might even be the prodigies of this generation from the Great Wilderness Sect.

These ten lined up side by side, blocking the Big Tiger ahead, raising heavy shields and sealing the path tightly.

The combined killing intent of the ten prodigies was wild and forceful, like a long rainbow piercing the sun.

Mo Hua’s expression turned cold.

The Big Tiger was enraged, its fur bristled in the wind, and black and white Monster Qi enveloped its body.

With a roar, the Big Tiger began its charge and attack.

The ten prodigies of the Great Wilderness Sect immediately engaged in a fierce battle with the human and tiger.

In just a few moments, the rancid-smelling tiger claws slashed at the heavy armor and shield, causing sparks to fly and leaving deep claw marks.

The Monster Qi and Blood Qi clashed, and neither side held back, the battle extremely fierce.

Mo Hua dared not delay any longer; if they got bogged down, the consequences would be unimaginable.

They had to break through as soon as possible!

In the blink of an eye, after another hundred rounds of battle.

Mo Hua used the Quicksand Skill to trap two of the heavily armored prodigies of the Great Wilderness Sect, and employed various spells such as Gold Light Skill, Fireball Technique, and Water Prison Technique to hold back the prodigies of the Great Wilderness Sect.

Then, the Big Tiger seized the opportunity to exert its hind legs, pushing off with a powerful stomp, cracking the ground.

The Big Tiger’s body shot out like a cannonball through a gap in the encirclement of the Great Wilderness Sect’s prodigies.

The ten prodigies of the Great Wilderness Sect could only watch helplessly as the Tiger Demon departed.

But just at that moment, one of the prodigies, evidently taller and more robust, suddenly displayed bulging muscles, blood-red savage patterns appeared, and he forcefully broke free from Mo Hua’s Water Prison Technique. With several leaps, he caught up and stood in front of the Big Tiger.

He was a tall young man.

The armor on his body was shattered, revealing crisscrossing grim scars beneath.

At this moment, the tall young man blocked the Big Tiger’s path.

The Big Tiger showed no mercy, attacking with a sweeping claw.

But this tall young man didn’t dodge or evade, his muscles seemingly turned to steel, sturdily stopping the Big Tiger’s claw.

The tiger claw embedded itself in his shoulder, leaving deep wounds.

Yet, the tall young man ignored the pain and, relying on his terrifying strength, desperately embraced the Big Tiger’s forepaw.

In a clash of flesh between human and demon, there was a momentary stalemate.

Although he was definitely no match for the fierce tiger, the fact that he could endure this long demonstrated his ferocity.

Mo Hua was startled.

This tall young man had such domineering strength.

But no one could block his path!

Mo Hua’s expression chilled, and he clasped his fingers, intending to eliminate the young man blocking the way. But when he looked up, he was suddenly stunned.

The chilling moonlight shone on the tall young man’s face.

A long scar ran down from his left face to his right cheek.

And the young man, with a determined and steady gaze, had an expression of ferocious violence and savagery, entirely devoid of the previous cheerfulness and kindness.

Mo Hua was taken aback, murmuring:

"Da’hu..."

The tall young man heard this and reacted as if struck by lightning, immediately looking up. He saw the familiar and handsome face on the tiger’s back.

This was a friend he had once been inseparable with, but whom he had now almost forgotten.

Memories of the past surged back.

The ferocity faded from the face of the tall young man, revealing a hint of pain and melancholia.

At this moment, a shout came from not far away:

"Meng Bohu, this is someone the Young Master wants, stop him with your life!"

The tall young man’s face turned pale at these words, casting a deep look at Mo Hua, then slowly releasing his grip and stepping aside, allowing the Big Tiger and Mo Hua to leave.

Mo Hua called out, "Da’hu!"
